title: Breach Remediation — Control Implementation Evidence Post-Incident

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

dataSources: `index=privacy` `sourcetype=hipaa:remediation_task` (`incident_id`, `control_id`, `target_date`), `index=o365` DLP rule hits, `index=ad` MFA coverage scripted metrics
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055)
spl:
index=privacy sourcetype="hipaa:remediation_task" earliest=-180d status!="Closed"
| eval due=strptime(target_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| join control_id [
    search index=metrics sourcetype="hipaa:control_metric" earliest=-1d
    | stats latest(metric_value) as current_value by control_id ]
| eval behind=if(now()>due AND current_value<100,1,0)
| where behind=1
| table incident_id, control_id, target_date, current_value, owner
value: Links post-breach corrective actions (MFA rollout, DLP rules) to measurable control telemetry so remediation is demonstrable, not narrative-only.
implementation: (1) Define `metric_value` as percent complete (e.g., MFA enrolled / total); (2) nightly scripted input; (3) board-level dashboard after major breaches.
visualization: Bullet chart (current vs target), Table (behind tasks).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
